description: The Windows Portable Executable format. Every runnable .exe and .dll on Windows must conform to this format. A compiled object file (.obj) produced by an assembler must have these structures added by the linker to become a valid executable.

variants.name: PE32 32-bit Console Executable (static, no DLL imports)
variants.description: Smallest possible Windows .exe: 32-bit, console subsystem, no explicit DLL imports. The loader always maps ntdll.dll, so even 'static' assembly programs typically call ExitProcess via kernel32. Suitable as a learning baseline.

required_components.name: MZ Header
required_components.description: MS-DOS 2.0 compatible header. Windows requires the MZ magic at file offset 0 and a valid e_lfanew pointing to the PE signature.

fields.name: (padding)
fields.note: Unused; set to zero for a minimal stub

fields.name: e_lfanew
fields.note: File offset of the PE signature; must be >= 0x40

code_example.source:
; === MZ Header (64 bytes at file offset 0x0000) ===
mz_header:
    dw 0x5A4D           ; e_magic = 'MZ'
    dw 0x0000           ; e_cblp  (unused)
    dw 0x0000           ; e_cp    (unused)
    dw 0x0000           ; e_crlc  (unused)
    dw 0x0000           ; e_cparhdr (unused)
    dw 0x0000           ; e_minalloc
    dw 0xFFFF           ; e_maxalloc
    dw 0x0000           ; e_ss
    dw 0x00B8           ; e_sp
    dw 0x0000           ; e_csum
    dw 0x0000           ; e_ip
    dw 0x0000           ; e_cs
    dw 0x0040           ; e_lfarlc
    dw 0x0000           ; e_ovno
    times 4 dw 0x0000   ; e_res[4]
    dw 0x0000           ; e_oemid
    dw 0x0000           ; e_oeminfo
    times 10 dw 0x0000  ; e_res2[10]
    dd 0x00000080       ; e_lfanew -> PE signature at 0x80

required_components.name: DOS Stub Program
required_components.description: Small 16-bit DOS program that prints 'This program cannot be run in DOS mode.' when run under DOS. Ignored by Windows. Pad with zeros if not needed.

code_example.source:
; === DOS Stub (64 bytes at file offset 0x0040) ===
; Can be all zeros; standard Microsoft stub shown for reference:
dos_stub:
    db 0x0E, 0x1F               ; push cs / pop ds
    db 0xBA, 0x0E, 0x00         ; mov dx, offset_of_message
    db 0xB4, 0x09               ; mov ah, 9 (print string)
    db 0xCD, 0x21               ; int 0x21
    db 0xB8, 0x01, 0x4C         ; mov ax, 0x4C01 (exit 1)
    db 0xCD, 0x21               ; int 0x21
    db 'This program cannot be run in DOS mode.', 13, 13, 10, '$'
    times (64 - ($ - dos_stub)) db 0

required_components.name: PE Signature
required_components.description: Four-byte magic at the file offset stored in e_lfanew. Must be exactly 'PE\0\0'.

fields.name: Signature
fields.note: ASCII 'PE' followed by two zero bytes

code_example.source:
; === PE Signature (4 bytes at e_lfanew = 0x0080) ===
pe_signature:
    db 'P', 'E', 0, 0     ; 'PE\0\0'

required_components.name: COFF File Header
required_components.description: 20-byte COFF header immediately after the PE signature. Identifies the target CPU, number of sections, and size of the Optional Header.

fields.name: NumberOfSections
fields.note: Count of Section Table entries; at minimum 1 (.text)

fields.name: TimeDateStamp
fields.note: Unix timestamp of link time; 0 is valid

code_example.source:
; === COFF File Header (20 bytes at 0x0084) ===
coff_header:
    dw 0x014C           ; Machine = IMAGE_FILE_MACHINE_I386
    dw NUM_SECTIONS     ; NumberOfSections
    dd 0                ; TimeDateStamp
    dd 0                ; PointerToSymbolTable
    dd 0                ; NumberOfSymbols
    dw 0x00E0           ; SizeOfOptionalHeader = 224 (PE32)
    dw 0x0102           ; Characteristics: EXECUTABLE_IMAGE | 32BIT_MACHINE

required_components.name: Optional Header (PE32)
required_components.description: 224-byte PE32 Optional Header. Required for all executables. Contains load address, entry point RVA, section alignments, subsystem, stack/heap sizes, and the 16-entry Data Directory array.

fields.name: BaseOfData
fields.note: RVA of .data (PE32 only; absent in PE32+)

fields.name: SizeOfImage
fields.note: Total image size in memory rounded to SectionAlignment

fields.name: SizeOfHeaders
fields.note: Size of all headers rounded to FileAlignment

fields.name: DataDirectory[0..15]
fields.note: 16 x (RVA:4 + Size:4); all zero for static executable

code_example.source:
; === Optional Header PE32 (224 bytes at 0x0098) ===
opt_header:
    dw 0x010B       ; Magic = PE32
    db 0, 0         ; LinkerVersion (major, minor)
    dd CODE_SIZE    ; SizeOfCode
    dd DATA_SIZE    ; SizeOfInitializedData
    dd 0            ; SizeOfUninitializedData
    dd ENTRY_RVA    ; AddressOfEntryPoint (RVA of _start)
    dd 0x1000       ; BaseOfCode (RVA)
    dd 0x2000       ; BaseOfData (RVA) -- PE32 only
    dd 0x00400000   ; ImageBase
    dd 0x1000       ; SectionAlignment (4 KB)
    dd 0x0200       ; FileAlignment    (512 B)
    dw 6, 0         ; OS version 6.0
    dw 0, 0         ; Image version
    dw 6, 0         ; Subsystem version 6.0
    dd 0            ; Win32VersionValue (must be 0)
    dd IMAGE_SIZE   ; SizeOfImage
    dd HDR_SIZE     ; SizeOfHeaders
    dd 0            ; CheckSum
    dw 3            ; Subsystem = WINDOWS_CUI
    dw 0x8140       ; DllCharacteristics
    dd 0x100000     ; SizeOfStackReserve
    dd 0x1000       ; SizeOfStackCommit
    dd 0x100000     ; SizeOfHeapReserve
    dd 0x1000       ; SizeOfHeapCommit
    dd 0            ; LoaderFlags
    dd 16           ; NumberOfRvaAndSizes
    times 16 dq 0   ; DataDirectory[0..15] = all zero (static)

required_components.name: Section Table Entry (.text)
required_components.description: One 40-byte section header for the executable code section.

fields.name: VirtualAddress
fields.note: RVA; must be multiple of SectionAlignment

code_example.source:
; === .text Section Table Entry (40 bytes) ===
text_shdr:
    db '.text', 0, 0, 0    ; Name (8 bytes)
    dd CODE_VSIZE           ; VirtualSize
    dd 0x1000               ; VirtualAddress (RVA)
    dd CODE_RAW             ; SizeOfRawData (aligned to 0x200)
    dd TEXT_FILE_OFFSET     ; PointerToRawData
    dd 0                    ; PointerToRelocations
    dd 0                    ; PointerToLinenumbers
    dw 0                    ; NumberOfRelocations
    dw 0                    ; NumberOfLinenumbers
    dd 0x60000020           ; Characteristics: CODE | READ | EXECUTE

required_components.name: Entry Point Code (_start)
required_components.description: First instruction executed. Must never return — must call ExitProcess or terminate via SYSCALL. Stack pointer is valid and 4-byte aligned on entry.

code_example.source:
; === Minimal 32-bit Entry Point ===
; Assemble: nasm -f win32 program.asm -o program.obj
; Link:     link program.obj kernel32.lib /subsystem:console /entry:_start /nodefaultlib
section .text
global  _start
extern  _ExitProcess@4     ; stdcall: 4-byte argument cleaned by callee

_start:
    push    0               ; exit code = 0
    call    _ExitProcess@4  ; terminates the process
    ; execution never reaches here

variants.name: PE32 32-bit Console Executable (dynamic, imports from kernel32.dll)
variants.description: 32-bit Windows console .exe that imports API functions from kernel32.dll via the Import Table mechanism. Adds an Import Directory, Import Lookup Table (ILT), Hint/Name Table, and Import Address Table (IAT) compared to the static variant.

required_components.name: MZ Header + DOS Stub + PE Signature + COFF File Header
required_components.description: Identical to pe32_console_static. See that variant for field details and code examples.

required_components.name: Optional Header (PE32) with populated Data Directories
required_components.description: Same layout as the static variant except DataDirectory[1] (Import Table) and DataDirectory[12] (IAT) must point to the import structures.

fields.name: DataDirectory[1].VirtualAddress
fields.note: RVA of the Import Directory Table

fields.name: DataDirectory[1].Size
fields.note: Total byte size of the Import Directory Table

fields.name: DataDirectory[12].VirtualAddress
fields.note: RVA of the Import Address Table (IAT)

fields.name: DataDirectory[12].Size
fields.note: Total byte size of the IAT

code_example.source:
; === DataDirectory array (128 bytes, inside Optional Header at offset 0x60) ===
data_dir:
    dq 0                            ; [0]  Export Table      (none)
    dd IMPORT_DIR_RVA               ; [1]  Import Table RVA
    dd IMPORT_DIR_SIZE              ;      Import Table Size
    times 10 dq 0                   ; [2..11] (unused)
    dd IAT_RVA                      ; [12] IAT RVA
    dd IAT_SIZE                     ;      IAT Size
    times 3 dq 0                    ; [13..15] (unused)

required_components.name: Import Directory Table
required_components.description: Array of IMAGE_IMPORT_DESCRIPTOR structures, one per imported DLL, terminated by a 20-byte all-zero entry.

fields.name: OriginalFirstThunk
fields.note: Import Lookup Table for this DLL

fields.name: FirstThunk
fields.note: Loader overwrites each entry with the real function address

code_example.source:
; === Import Directory Table (.idata section) ===
import_dir:
    ; Descriptor for KERNEL32.DLL
    dd ILT_KERNEL32_RVA     ; OriginalFirstThunk -> ILT
    dd 0                    ; TimeDateStamp
    dd 0xFFFFFFFF           ; ForwarderChain
    dd DLL_NAME_RVA         ; Name -> 'KERNEL32.DLL\0'
    dd IAT_KERNEL32_RVA     ; FirstThunk -> IAT (rewritten at load time)
    ; Null terminator (all zeros = end of table)
    times 5 dd 0
import_dir_end:

; Import Lookup Table (ILT) — same layout as IAT before binding
ilt_kernel32:
    dd HINT_EXITPROCESS_RVA ; -> Hint/Name for ExitProcess
    dd 0                    ; null terminator

; Import Address Table (IAT) — loader overwrites at runtime
iat_kernel32:
    dd HINT_EXITPROCESS_RVA ; same as ILT initially
    dd 0                    ; null terminator
iat_end:

; Hint/Name entry for ExitProcess
hint_exitprocess:
    dw 0                    ; hint (0 = perform full name search)
    db 'ExitProcess', 0     ; function name (null-terminated)

dll_name_kernel32:
    db 'KERNEL32.DLL', 0

required_components.name: Entry Point Code (32-bit, with DLL imports)
required_components.description: Entry point that calls imported functions through the IAT. Indirect call through the IAT thunk slot.

code_example.source:
; === 32-bit Entry Point calling kernel32 via IAT ===
section .text
global  _start
extern  _ExitProcess@4

_start:
    push    0
    call    _ExitProcess@4

; ---- Hello World variant ----
section .data
    msg     db 'Hello, World!', 13, 10
    msglen  equ $ - msg

section .text
global  _hello
extern  _GetStdHandle@4
extern  _WriteConsoleA@20
extern  _ExitProcess@4

_hello:
    ; GetStdHandle(STD_OUTPUT_HANDLE = -11)
    push    -11
    call    _GetStdHandle@4         ; EAX = handle

    ; WriteConsoleA(hOut, msg, len, &written, NULL)
    push    0                       ; lpReserved = NULL
    push    0                       ; placeholder for lpNumberOfCharsWritten
    lea     ecx, [esp]              ; &written
    push    ecx
    push    msglen
    push    msg
    push    eax                     ; hConsoleOutput
    call    _WriteConsoleA@20

    push    0
    call    _ExitProcess@4

variants.name: PE32 32-bit GUI Executable (dynamic, WINDOWS_GUI subsystem)
variants.description: 32-bit Windows GUI application: no console window allocated. Subsystem set to 2 (WINDOWS_GUI). Typically imports from kernel32.dll, user32.dll, and gdi32.dll. Entry point is conventionally WinMain.

required_components.name: All components from pe32_console_dynamic
required_components.description: Identical structure. Only the Subsystem field value and entry point convention differ.

required_components.name: Subsystem field = WINDOWS_GUI
required_components.description: Change the Subsystem word from 3 (CUI) to 2 (GUI). No console window will be created.

code_example.source:
; In the Optional Header, set:
    dw 2    ; Subsystem = WINDOWS_GUI  (was 3 for console)

required_components.name: WinMain Entry Point (32-bit GUI)
required_components.description: GUI apps enter at WinMain. Stack on entry: [esp+4]=hInstance, [esp+8]=hPrevInstance, [esp+12]=lpCmdLine, [esp+16]=nCmdShow.

code_example.source:
; === 32-bit GUI WinMain Entry Point ===
section .data
    msg     db 'Hello from SASM!', 0
    caption db 'SASM', 0

section .text
global  WinMain
extern  _MessageBoxA@16
extern  _ExitProcess@4

WinMain:
    ; MessageBoxA(NULL, msg, caption, MB_OK)
    push    0               ; uType = MB_OK
    push    caption
    push    msg
    push    0               ; hWnd = NULL
    call    _MessageBoxA@16
    push    0
    call    _ExitProcess@4

variants.name: PE32+ 64-bit Console Executable (static, no DLL imports)
variants.description: 64-bit Windows .exe using PE32+ format (magic 0x020B). BaseOfData field is absent. ImageBase and stack/heap sizes are 8 bytes. Machine is 0x8664 (AMD64). RSP is 16-byte aligned on entry.

required_components.name: COFF File Header (PE32+)
required_components.description: Same layout as PE32 but Machine = 0x8664, Characteristics omits 32BIT_MACHINE, and SizeOfOptionalHeader = 240.

code_example.source:
coff_header:
    dw 0x8664           ; Machine = IMAGE_FILE_MACHINE_AMD64
    dw NUM_SECTIONS
    dd 0                ; TimeDateStamp
    dd 0                ; PointerToSymbolTable
    dd 0                ; NumberOfSymbols
    dw 0x00F0           ; SizeOfOptionalHeader = 240 (PE32+)
    dw 0x0022           ; Characteristics: EXECUTABLE | LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE

required_components.name: Optional Header (PE32+)
required_components.description: 240-byte PE32+ Optional Header. BaseOfData field is absent. ImageBase, stack, and heap size fields are 8 bytes (vs 4 bytes in PE32).

fields.name: BaseOfData
fields.note: ABSENT — does not exist in PE32+

code_example.source:
; === Optional Header PE32+ (240 bytes at 0x0098) ===
opt_header:
    dw 0x020B               ; Magic = PE32+
    db 0, 0                 ; LinkerVersion
    dd CODE_SIZE            ; SizeOfCode
    dd DATA_SIZE            ; SizeOfInitializedData
    dd 0                    ; SizeOfUninitializedData
    dd ENTRY_RVA            ; AddressOfEntryPoint
    dd 0x1000               ; BaseOfCode
    ; Note: no BaseOfData field in PE32+
    dq 0x0000000140000000   ; ImageBase (8 bytes)
    dd 0x1000               ; SectionAlignment
    dd 0x0200               ; FileAlignment
    dw 6, 0                 ; OS version 6.0
    dw 0, 0                 ; Image version
    dw 6, 0                 ; Subsystem version
    dd 0                    ; Win32VersionValue (must be 0)
    dd IMAGE_SIZE           ; SizeOfImage
    dd HDR_SIZE             ; SizeOfHeaders
    dd 0                    ; CheckSum
    dw 3                    ; Subsystem = WINDOWS_CUI
    dw 0x8160               ; DllCharacteristics
    dq 0x100000             ; SizeOfStackReserve (8 bytes)
    dq 0x1000               ; SizeOfStackCommit  (8 bytes)
    dq 0x100000             ; SizeOfHeapReserve  (8 bytes)
    dq 0x1000               ; SizeOfHeapCommit   (8 bytes)
    dd 0                    ; LoaderFlags
    dd 16                   ; NumberOfRvaAndSizes
    times 16 dq 0           ; DataDirectory[0..15]

required_components.name: Entry Point Code (64-bit, static)
required_components.description: 64-bit entry point. RSP is 16-byte aligned on kernel entry. The Microsoft x64 ABI requires 32-byte shadow space before any CALL. Must not return.

code_example.source:
; === Minimal 64-bit Entry Point (requires kernel32.lib even for 'static') ===
; Assemble: nasm -f win64 program.asm -o program.obj
; Link:     link program.obj kernel32.lib /subsystem:console /entry:start /nodefaultlib
section .text
global  start
extern  ExitProcess

start:
    sub     rsp, 40         ; 32-byte shadow space + 8-byte align padding
    xor     ecx, ecx        ; exit code = 0  (first arg in RCX)
    call    ExitProcess     ; does not return
    ; execution never reaches here

variants.name: PE32+ 64-bit Console Executable (dynamic, imports from kernel32.dll)
variants.description: 64-bit Windows console .exe that imports from kernel32.dll. Uses Microsoft x64 calling convention: first four integer arguments in RCX, RDX, R8, R9 with a mandatory 32-byte shadow space on the stack before every CALL. ILT/IAT entries are 8 bytes each.

required_components.name: All PE32+ header structures
required_components.description: Same as pe32plus_console_static plus the Import Table data directories and import structures (see pe32_console_dynamic for layout; ILT/IAT entries are 8 bytes in PE32+).

required_components.name: 64-bit ILT / IAT Entry
required_components.description: Import Lookup Table and Import Address Table entries are 8 bytes in PE32+. Bit 63 = 0: RVA of Hint/Name structure. Bit 63 = 1: low 16 bits = ordinal number.

fields.name: Entry (by name)
fields.note: Bit 63 = 0; lower 31 bits = RVA of Hint/Name

code_example.source:
; === 64-bit ILT/IAT entries (8 bytes each) ===
ilt_kernel32:
    dq HINT_EXITPROCESS_RVA     ; bit 63 = 0: by-name import
    dq 0                        ; null terminator

iat_kernel32:
    dq HINT_EXITPROCESS_RVA     ; replaced at load time with real VA
    dq 0

required_components.name: Entry Point Code (64-bit, dynamic)
required_components.description: 64-bit entry point using Microsoft x64 ABI. Must allocate 32-byte shadow space before every CALL.

code_example.source:
; === 64-bit Console Entry Point (Hello World) ===
section .data
    msg     db 'Hello, World!', 13, 10
    msglen  equ $ - msg
    written dq 0

section .text
global  start
extern  GetStdHandle
extern  WriteConsoleA
extern  ExitProcess

start:
    sub     rsp, 56             ; 32-byte shadow + 2 extra slots + 8-byte align

    ; GetStdHandle(STD_OUTPUT_HANDLE = -11)  ->  RAX = hStdOut
    mov     ecx, -11
    call    GetStdHandle
    mov     rbx, rax            ; save handle

    ; WriteConsoleA(hOut, buf, len, pWritten, NULL)
    mov     rcx, rbx            ; hConsoleOutput
    lea     rdx, [rel msg]      ; lpBuffer
    mov     r8d, msglen         ; nNumberOfCharsToWrite
    lea     r9,  [rel written]  ; lpNumberOfCharsWritten
    mov     qword [rsp+32], 0   ; 5th arg: lpReserved = NULL
    call    WriteConsoleA

    ; ExitProcess(0)
    xor     ecx, ecx
    call    ExitProcess

variants.name: PE32+ 64-bit GUI Executable (dynamic, WINDOWS_GUI subsystem)
variants.description: 64-bit Windows GUI application. No console window. Entry point is conventionally WinMain (hInstance, hPrevInstance, lpCmdLine, nCmdShow) passed in RCX, RDX, R8, R9 per the Microsoft x64 ABI.

required_components.name: All PE32+ header structures (same as pe32plus_console_dynamic)
required_components.description: Identical structure. Only the Subsystem field value and entry point differ.

required_components.name: Subsystem field = WINDOWS_GUI
required_components.description: Set Subsystem to 2 (WINDOWS_GUI) in the Optional Header.

code_example.source:
; In the Optional Header, change:
    dw 2    ; Subsystem = WINDOWS_GUI

required_components.name: WinMain Entry Point (64-bit GUI)
required_components.description: 64-bit GUI entry point. Arguments arrive in registers per Microsoft x64 ABI: RCX=hInstance, RDX=hPrevInstance, R8=lpCmdLine, R9D=nCmdShow.

code_example.source:
; === 64-bit GUI WinMain Entry Point ===
section .data
    msg     db 'Hello from SASM!', 0
    caption db 'SASM', 0

section .text
global  WinMain
extern  MessageBoxA
extern  ExitProcess

WinMain:
    sub     rsp, 40             ; shadow space + alignment
    xor     ecx, ecx            ; hWnd = NULL
    lea     rdx, [rel msg]      ; lpText
    lea     r8,  [rel caption]  ; lpCaption
    xor     r9d, r9d            ; uType = MB_OK
    call    MessageBoxA
    xor     ecx, ecx
    call    ExitProcess
